text.title: Go worker pool: bounded concurrent job execution with context and panic recovery
text.summary: A long-lived worker pool for consuming queued jobs with bounded parallelism, context cancellation, and panic recovery. Use when jobs are long-lived and backpressure on Submit is acceptable.

section.title: Worker pool implementation

rule.statement: Worker goroutines must: (1) select on both ctx.Done() and the jobs channel, (2) recover panics with defer/recover and send to an error channel or logger, (3) call wg.Done() in a deferred call so it runs even on panic.
rule.rationale: Without ctx.Done() selection, goroutines leak when the context is cancelled. Without panic recovery, one bad job takes the whole process down. Deferred wg.Done() ensures the WaitGroup counter is correct even after a panic.

section.reference: Full worker pool with context, panic recovery, and error channel.

rule.statement: Size the jobs buffer to match the upstream burst rate, not to an arbitrary constant. Callers must select on ctx.Done() when submitting to avoid blocking forever if the pool is full.
rule.rationale: An unbuffered or undersized channel causes Submit to block under burst load, potentially deadlocking if the caller cannot cancel. An oversized buffer hides backpressure problems.

antipattern.reason: The caller cannot know whether jobs succeeded. Wire to an error channel or at minimum a structured logger with the job identifier.
